skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This skill provides a wrapper for the inference.sh CLI to generate AI videos. All detected patterns (external_commands, network URLs, cryptographic references) are false positives - the skill contains only documentation examples and legitimate API endpoint references. No malicious behavior detected. The pipe-to-shell install pattern is standard for CLI tools with documented manual alternatives.

medium_findings.title: Pipe-to-Shell Install Pattern
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation shows `curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh` for CLI installation. While this is a common pattern, piping directly to shell carries inherent risk. However, the skill provides manual installation alternatives and the service is a legitimate AI video generation platform.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard CLI install pattern used by many legitimate tools. Manual alternatives documented. Low actual risk for well-known CLI tools.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Veo and 40+ Models
content.value_statement: Create professional AI-generated videos using Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok models directly from Claude Code. Generate text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, and avatar animations without leaving your terminal.
actual_capabilities: Generate text-to-video using Google Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance, and Grok models
Create image-to-video animations from static images using Wan 2.5
Produce AI avatar videos with lipsync from portrait photos and audio
Upscale video quality and add foley sound effects
Access 40+ video models including OmniHuman, Fabric, and HunyuanVideo
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and API access credits
Video generation is asynchronous and may take minutes to complete
Output quality depends on the underlying AI model capabilities
Network connectivity required for all video generation operations

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: Quickly generate engaging video content for social media posts, reels, and short-form videos using text prompts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, social media managers, marketers

use_cases.title: Product Demo Generator
use_cases.description: Create product demonstration videos and explainer content from text descriptions without video production expertise.

use_cases.title: AI Avatar Presenter
use_cases.description: Generate talking head videos with realistic lip-sync from portrait photos and voice narration for training or presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video of a sunset over mountains with clouds moving slowly using Veo 3.1 Fast model
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this portrait photo to make the person slightly turn their head. Use the Wan 2.5 model with the image from ~/photos/portrait.jpg
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting static images into moving video

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Voice
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AI avatar video using the portrait at ~/images/speaker.jpg and narration from ~/audio/intro.mp3. Use OmniHuman 1.5 for multi-character support.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating talking head videos with lip-sync

prompt_templates.title: Video Post-Production
prompt_templates.prompt: Upscale the video at ~/videos/raw footage.mp4 to 4K quality using Topaz Video Upscaler, then add foley sound effects like footsteps and wind using HunyuanVideo Foley.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enhancing and adding audio to existing videos

output_examples.input: Generate a video of a drone shot flying over a forest canopy at golden hour
Create a talking head video from my portrait photo with voiceover
output_examples.output: AI avatar video generated at ~/videos/avatar-intro.mp4 with synchronized lip movement

best_practices: Write clear, specific prompts describing the visual scene, camera movement, and mood for best results
Use appropriate models for each task: Veo for quality, Wan for image animation, OmniHuman for avatars
Check generation status with polling or streaming to handle long-running video jobs
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make a cool video' - be specific about subject, movement, and style
Do not expect real-time generation - video AI requires processing time
Avoid using copyrighted images or audio without proper licensing for avatar generation

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign up at inference.sh and purchase credits. The skill uses the infsh CLI which requires authentication.

faq.question: Which model is best for text-to-video?
faq.answer: Google Veo 3.1 offers the best quality with optional audio. Veo 3.1 Fast is quicker for prototyping. Seedance 1.5 Pro offers first-frame control.

faq.question: Can I generate videos from my own images?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Wan 2.5 model for image-to-video. Provide an image_url pointing to your image hosted online.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and duration. Fast models take 1-3 minutes, quality models may take 5-10 minutes.

faq.question: Can I add audio to silent videos?
faq.answer: Yes, use HunyuanVideo Foley to add sound effects, or provide audio_url with image_url for avatar videos to include narration.

faq.question: Is this skill available for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools as specified in the allowed-tools configuration.
